note: Named user types for swimchain QA study rounds. P0 is the program's acceptance gate: if Diane cannot get online unassisted, we are not done. Specificity is deliberate - it drives honest, divergent feedback.

personas.name: Diane, 63 - The Mom Test
personas.profile: Retired nurse. Windows laptop, Chrome, default settings. Received a download link from her son via text message. Vocabulary: none of 'wallet', 'keys', 'PoW', 'node', 'sponsorship'. Patience: two confusing moments, then she closes the app and calls her son.
tasks: Open the link, download, install, launch - counting every step and every question the app asks
Get from first launch to 'online' without help (create identity should not mention seeds or keys)
Find her son and send him a message (invite-link flow if present, otherwise document the wall)
Reply to a message she receives; react to one
Close and reopen the app the next day - is she still logged in and can she find the conversation?
personas.headline_metric: unassisted time-to-first-message (target: under 10 minutes, zero external help)

personas.name: Nadia, 28 - Cautious Newcomer
personas.profile: Grad student, phone-first, privacy-curious. Reads every dialog before clicking. Will abandon anything that asks for personal information without explaining why.
tasks: Evaluate swimchain.io: does she understand what this is and why decay matters within 60 seconds?
Onboard in feed-client: identity creation, what the backup prompt asks of her, whether sponsorship status is comprehensible
Find a space to follow, read, then make her first post (narrate the PoW wait: does the UI explain it?)
Try the report flow on something - does she trust what happened?

personas.name: Theo, 19 - Power Poster
personas.profile: Posts constantly, impatient, will find edge cases by accident. Cares about reactions, streaks, visible momentum. Will paste emoji, huge text, and double-click every button.
tasks: Rapid-fire posting in feed-client: how does PoW feel at volume? Does anything double-submit?
React to 20 things; watch the decay/heat UI - does keeping content alive feel like a game or a chore?
Try to break composers: emoji, 10k chars, markdown, empty posts, rapid edits
Live-update check: second window posts, does his feed show the new-posts pill without refresh?

personas.name: Priya, 33 - Community Builder
personas.profile: Ex-Discord moderator migrating a 40-person community. Compares everything to Discord. Needs invites, member management, and moderation to feel one-click.
tasks: Create a public space and a private channel in chat-client; invite a second identity; the invitee accepts - full round trip
Member panel: list, roles, kick someone - did the key rotation happen invisibly?
Moderate: report content, block a user, verify the blocked user's content disappears for her
Sponsorship: create an offer for a newcomer, approve their claim - could she onboard her 40 people this way?

personas.name: Marcus, 41 - Node Operator
personas.profile: Sysadmin, terminal-fluent, skeptical of Electron-anything. Reads logs. Will judge the project by whether the node lifecycle is honest (real states, real errors).
tasks: Desktop bundle: install, watch node spawn, read the status bar - do peer counts and sync states look truthful?
Switch networks (testnet -> regtest): does identity/data separation behave as documented?
Kill the node process externally - how do the embedded clients degrade? Any fake data?
Check the docs/developers page against reality: run the regtest quickstart verbatim

personas.name: Elaine, 57 - Reader
personas.profile: Lurker. Never posts. Browser zoom at 125%, sensitive to low contrast and small tap targets. Uses search as her front door.
tasks: Read-only journey: web-gateway pages (no identity) - can she browse spaces and read threads with the node offline notice never lying to her?
search-client: find a topic, follow deep links to the content - do they land where they promise?
Readability audit at 125% zoom: clipped text, contrast, focus states in feed and forum
Does anything demand an identity just to read? Note every gate she hits

personas.name: Dmitri, 36 - Privacy Skeptic
personas.profile: Journalist. Assumes surveillance until convinced otherwise. Reads what's on the wire (devtools network tab open the whole session). DMs and private spaces are his entire use case.
tasks: DM round trip in feed-client: request, accept on second identity, exchange messages - then inspect: is ciphertext actually on the wire?
Private channel in chat: create, invite, post - can a non-member identity read anything?
What leaks in cleartext? (space names, message timing, identity linkage) - document honestly
Kick a member from a private channel, then verify the kicked identity truly can't read new messages (key rotation reality check)

personas.name: Kofi, 24 - Preservationist
personas.profile: Archivist temperament. Hates losing things. Runs archiver-client and analytics side by side, sets budgets, and expects numbers to be real.
tasks: Archiver: find at-risk content, engage to rescue it, verify the pool status came from the node (not local math)
Set a PoW budget, exhaust it, confirm the engine stops and the meter is honest
Analytics: do engagementsLast24h and active-swimmer numbers move when he acts? Cross-check one number by hand
Leave both running an hour - memory, errors, drift

personas.name: AXIOM - Machine Consumer
personas.profile: Headless integrator. No UI, no patience, no charity. Probes the JSON-RPC surface and web-gateway HTTP endpoints exactly as documented and reports every deviation.
tasks: Probe documented RPC methods (get_info, list_spaces, search, get_content, submit_* auth behavior) - status, shape, timing for each
web-gateway: /api/health, /api/search/*, sitemap.xml - correct content-types, charsets, cache behavior
Error contract: bogus params, missing auth, unknown ids - are errors typed and honest?
WS /ws endpoint: handshake, subscribe, receive one real event, reconnect behavior
